explanation:
**Official registration procedures** in Argentina have costs, timelines, and requirements set by different agencies (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Immigration, Civil Registry, etc.). This calculator estimates your total cost and required documents.

## How It's Calculated

Register a new car in Argentina by obtaining your green and blue registration cards and ownership title. This involves DNRPA federal fees plus provincial surcharges.

This calculator applies the formula using your vehicle value and province, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference estimate only. For critical decisions (financial, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and updated regularly.

faq.a: Dirección Nacional de Registros Propiedad Automotor — Argentina's National Directorate of Motor Vehicle Registration.

faq.q: How much does it cost to register a new car in Argentina?
faq.a: Costs vary by vehicle value and province. The fee includes a federal fixed portion plus a percentage-based portion, plus provincial surcharges.

faq.q: What's the difference between a green card and blue card?
faq.a: Green card is proof of ownership; blue card is proof of registration. Both are required for new vehicle registration.

faq.q: Do I need an appointment to register my car?
faq.a: Yes, most DNRPA offices require an appointment. Schedule online through argentina.gob.ar or your province's DNRPA website.

faq.q: How long does car registration take in Argentina?
faq.a: Typically 2–5 business days with a gestor (licensed agent), or longer if handling it yourself. Express service is available for an additional fee.

faq.q: What documents do I need to register a new car?
faq.a: You'll need the purchase invoice, seller's ID, proof of address, vehicle inspection, and insurance proof. Requirements vary slightly by province.

faq.q: Can I register my car online?
faq.a: Most of the process can be started online, but you must visit a DNRPA office in person to complete registration and receive your cards.

faq.q: Do leasing companies register cars differently?
faq.a: Yes. In lease agreements, the leasing company typically covers part of the registration fee, and both lender and buyer may appear on documents.

faq.q: Should I hire a gestor to register my car?
faq.a: Many people do to save time and avoid bureaucracy. Gestores handle paperwork and appointments, but registration is straightforward if you do it yourself.
